title: Vilfa lenta
taxonomy: Vilfa lenta is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This classification makes it a type of grass. More specifically, Vilfa lenta is a member of the genus Vilfa, which is a group of grasses native to South America. The exact classification of Vilfa lenta is within the subfamily Pooideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Poeae.

intro: Vilfa lenta, also known as Gentianella lutea or Yellow gentian, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Vilfa lenta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Vilfa lenta is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as digestive problems.
